a_anchor: The information shall be provided in writing, or by other means, including, where appropriate, by electronic means

notes: A is about giving information to people in writing; B is about how long data may be stored. Different activities, nothing to clash. The only shared concept is "personal data", which sits on nearly every statement.

a_anchor: The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller the erasure of personal data concerning him or her

a_condition: without undue delay where one of the following grounds applies

a_anchor: The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller the erasure of personal data concerning him or her
b_anchor: not further processed in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes
a_condition: without undue delay where one of the following grounds applies

a_anchor: The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller the erasure of personal data concerning him or her
b_anchor: personal data may be stored for longer periods insofar as the personal data will be processed solely for archiving purposes
a_condition: without undue delay where one of the following grounds applies
b_condition: insofar as the personal data will be processed solely for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes in accordance with Article 89(1) subject to implementation of the appropriate technical and organisational measures required by this Regulation in order to safeguard the rights and freedoms of the data subject
notes: A says the controller MUST DELETE data when someone asks and a valid ground applies. B says the controller MAY KEEP data longer for archiving. Delete vs keep on the same data, and both can apply at the same time - read on their own, these two pull in opposite directions, so I labelled the tension. The regulation does fix this elsewhere (Art 17(3)(d): the deletion right does not apply to archiving - and that statement is in the graph as art_17/par_3), but our rule is to judge the two statements alone. If you read B's "solely for archiving purposes" wording as already making the clash invisible face-on, overrule to none. Note: the detector said none, so confirming this charges it a miss.

a_anchor: The data subject shall have the right to object... to processing... based on point (e) or (f) of Article 6(1)

b_condition: the data subject has given explicit consent to the processing of those personal data for one or more specified purposes, except where Union or Member State law provide that the prohibition referred to in paragraph 1 may not be lifted by the data subject

a_anchor: The data subject shall have the right to object... to processing... based on point (e) or (f) of Article 6(1)

a_anchor: The data subject shall have the right to object... to processing... based on point (e) or (f) of Article 6(1)

a_anchor: collected for specified, explicit and legitimate purposes
b_anchor: not further processed in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes
b_condition: in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes
notes: s1 and s3 are the two complementary halves of purpose limitation (collect-for-purposes / no incompatible processing)

a_anchor: collected for specified, explicit and legitimate purposes
b_anchor: personal data may be stored for longer periods insofar as the personal data will be processed solely for archiving purposes
b_condition: insofar as the personal data will be processed solely for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes in accordance with Article 89(1) subject to implementation of the appropriate technical and organisational measures required by this Regulation in order to safeguard the rights and freedoms of the data subject
notes: A says purposes must be specified up front; B allows longer storage. Both can be true at once, no clash.

a_anchor: further processing for archiving purposes... shall... not be considered to be incompatible with the initial purposes
b_anchor: not further processed in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes
a_condition: for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes and in accordance with Article 89(1)
b_condition: in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes
notes: confirmed: s2 (compatible further processing for archiving/research) carves out s3's prohibition

a_anchor: not further processed in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes
b_anchor: personal data may be stored for longer periods insofar as the personal data will be processed solely for archiving purposes
a_condition: in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes
b_condition: insofar as the personal data will be processed solely for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes in accordance with Article 89(1) subject to implementation of the appropriate technical and organisational measures required by this Regulation in order to safeguard the rights and freedoms of the data subject
notes: A bans further processing that is INCOMPATIBLE with the original purposes. B's archiving is not visibly incompatible - Art 5(1)(b) itself says archiving counts as compatible (that carve-out is the pt_b#s2 permission).

a_anchor: not further processed in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes
b_anchor: kept in a form which permits identification of data subjects for no longer than is necessary
a_condition: in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes
b_condition: in a form which permits identification of data subjects for no longer than is necessary for the purposes for which the personal data are processed
notes: Both statements restrict processing - one bans incompatible re-use, the other limits how long data is kept. They point the same way; nothing to clash.

a_anchor: not further processed in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes
b_anchor: the data subject has given consent to the processing of his or her personal data for one or more specific purposes
a_condition: in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes
b_condition: the data subject has given consent to the processing of his or her personal data for one or more specific purposes
notes: consent permission defines the purposes the purpose-limitation prohibition presupposes - compatible

a_anchor: not further processed in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes
b_anchor: the data subject has given explicit consent to the processing... except where Union or Member State law provide that the prohibition... may not be lifted
a_condition: in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes
b_condition: the data subject has given explicit consent to the processing of those personal data for one or more specified purposes, except where Union or Member State law provide that the prohibition referred to in paragraph 1 may not be lifted by the data subject
notes: 9(2) derogation vs purpose limitation - independent dimensions (legal basis vs purpose compatibility)

a_anchor: not further processed in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes
b_anchor: processing is necessary for the purposes of preventive or occupational medicine, medical diagnosis, health or social care
a_condition: in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes
b_condition: necessary for the purposes of preventive or occupational medicine, for the assessment of the working capacity of the employee, medical diagnosis, the provision of health or social care or treatment or the management of health or social care systems and services on the basis of Union or Member State law or pursuant to contract with a health professional and subject to the conditions and safeguards referred to in paragraph 3
notes: as row 61

a_anchor: not further processed in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes
b_anchor: processing is necessary for archiving purposes... based on Union or Member State law which shall be proportionate... and provide for suitable and specific measures
a_condition: in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes
b_condition: processing is necessary for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes in accordance with Article 89(1) based on Union or Member State law which shall be proportionate to the aim pursued, respect the essence of the right to data protection and provide for suitable and specific measures to safeguard the fundamental rights and the interests of the data subject
notes: as row 61

a_anchor: personal data may be stored for longer periods insofar as the personal data will be processed solely for archiving purposes
b_anchor: kept in a form which permits identification of data subjects for no longer than is necessary
a_condition: insofar as the personal data will be processed solely for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes in accordance with Article 89(1) subject to implementation of the appropriate technical and organisational measures required by this Regulation in order to safeguard the rights and freedoms of the data subject
b_condition: in a form which permits identification of data subjects for no longer than is necessary for the purposes for which the personal data are processed
notes: B sets the storage time limit; A is the same paragraph's own exception to it (longer storage allowed for archiving). A even references B. Exactly what this label is for.

a_anchor: the data subject shall have the right to have incomplete personal data completed, including by means of providing a supplementary statement
b_anchor: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin... or sexual orientation shall be prohibited.
a_condition: Taking into account the purposes of the processing, including by means of providing a supplementary statement
notes: prima facie: completion is a processing operation on data subsumed by 9(1)'s prohibited class (PersonalData > SpecialCategory); resolvable via 9(2) exception structures in-graph

a_anchor: The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller without undue delay the rectification of inaccurate personal data
b_anchor: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin... or sexual orientation shall be prohibited.
a_condition: without undue delay
notes: prima facie: rectification is a processing operation on data subsumed by 9(1)'s prohibited class; resolvable via 9(2) exception structures in-graph

a_anchor: The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller the erasure of personal data concerning him or her
b_anchor: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin... or sexual orientation shall be prohibited.
a_condition: without undue delay where one of the following grounds applies
notes: prima facie: erasure is a processing operation on data subsumed by 9(1)'s prohibited class; resolvable via 9(2) exception structures in-graph

a_anchor: the pseudonymisation and encryption of personal data
b_anchor: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin... or sexual orientation shall be prohibited.
a_condition: Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk
notes: prima facie: obliged/permitted processing act on data contradicted by 9(1)'s unconditional prohibited class

a_anchor: the ability to restore the availability and access to personal data in a timely manner
b_anchor: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin... or sexual orientation shall be prohibited.
a_condition: in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident
notes: data accessibility vs data processing prohibition - irrelevant

a_anchor: the controller shall ... notify the personal data breach to the supervisory authority
b_anchor: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin... or sexual orientation shall be prohibited.
a_condition: In the case of a personal data breach, without undue delay and, where feasible, not later than 72 hours after having become aware of it, unless the personal data breach is unlikely to result in a risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons
notes: breach notification vs data processing prohibition - irrelevant

a_anchor: the controller shall communicate the personal data breach to the data subject without undue delay
b_anchor: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin... or sexual orientation shall be prohibited.
a_condition: When the personal data breach is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons
notes: breach notification vs data processing prohibition - irrelevant

a_anchor: the controller shall, prior to the processing, carry out an assessment of the impact of the envisaged processing operations
b_anchor: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin... or sexual orientation shall be prohibited.
a_condition: Where a type of processing in particular using new technologies, and taking into account the nature, scope, context and purposes of the processing, is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, prior to the processing
notes: DPIA duty vs data processing prohibition - compatible

a_anchor: processed lawfully, fairly and in a transparent manner in relation to the data subject
b_anchor: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin... or sexual orientation shall be prohibited.
notes: prima facie: obliged/permitted processing act on data subsumed by 9(1)'s unconditional prohibited class; resolved in-graph via 9(2) EXCEPTION_OF (consistent application of the pair-local standard)

a_anchor: collected for specified, explicit and legitimate purposes
b_anchor: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin... or sexual orientation shall be prohibited.
notes: prima facie: obliged/permitted processing act on data subsumed by 9(1)'s unconditional prohibited class; resolved in-graph via 9(2) EXCEPTION_OF (consistent application of the pair-local standard)

a_anchor: further processing for archiving purposes... shall... not be considered to be incompatible with the initial purposes
b_anchor: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin... or sexual orientation shall be prohibited.
a_condition: for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes and in accordance with Article 89(1)
notes: prima facie: obliged/permitted processing act on data subsumed by 9(1)'s unconditional prohibited class; resolved in-graph via 9(2) EXCEPTION_OF (consistent application of the pair-local standard)

a_anchor: personal data may be stored for longer periods insofar as the personal data will be processed solely for archiving purposes
b_anchor: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin... or sexual orientation shall be prohibited.
a_condition: insofar as the personal data will be processed solely for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes in accordance with Article 89(1) subject to implementation of the appropriate technical and organisational measures required by this Regulation in order to safeguard the rights and freedoms of the data subject
notes: Keeping/storing data IS a form of processing, and 9(1) bans ALL processing of special-category data, unconditionally. So if the stored data is special-category, A permits something B bans. Same shape as the other 9(1) rows you kept as candidate_contradiction. Already auto-resolved through the 9(2) exception edges.

a_anchor: kept in a form which permits identification of data subjects for no longer than is necessary
b_anchor: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin... or sexual orientation shall be prohibited.
a_condition: in a form which permits identification of data subjects for no longer than is necessary for the purposes for which the personal data are processed
notes: Same as above: the duty to keep data in limited form is still processing, which 9(1) bans outright for special-category data. Same shape as the kept 9(1) rows; auto-resolved via 9(2).

a_anchor: the data subject has given consent to the processing of his or her personal data for one or more specific purposes
b_anchor: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin... or sexual orientation shall be prohibited.
a_condition: the data subject has given consent to the processing of his or her personal data for one or more specific purposes
notes: prima facie: 6(1)(a) permission covers special categories on its face; layered norm resolved by 9(2)(a) (lex specialis) - resolution lives in the exception edges, not the pair

a_anchor: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin... or sexual orientation shall be prohibited.
b_anchor: the data subject has given explicit consent to the processing... except where Union or Member State law provide that the prohibition... may not be lifted
b_condition: the data subject has given explicit consent to the processing of those personal data for one or more specified purposes, except where Union or Member State law provide that the prohibition referred to in paragraph 1 may not be lifted by the data subject
notes: confirmed: 9(2)(a) derogates 9(1)

a_anchor: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin... or sexual orientation shall be prohibited.
b_anchor: processing is necessary for the purposes of preventive or occupational medicine, medical diagnosis, health or social care
b_condition: necessary for the purposes of preventive or occupational medicine, for the assessment of the working capacity of the employee, medical diagnosis, the provision of health or social care or treatment or the management of health or social care systems and services on the basis of Union or Member State law or pursuant to contract with a health professional and subject to the conditions and safeguards referred to in paragraph 3
notes: confirmed: 9(2)(h) derogates 9(1)

a_anchor: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin... or sexual orientation shall be prohibited.
b_anchor: processing is necessary for archiving purposes... based on Union or Member State law which shall be proportionate... and provide for suitable and specific measures
b_condition: processing is necessary for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes in accordance with Article 89(1) based on Union or Member State law which shall be proportionate to the aim pursued, respect the essence of the right to data protection and provide for suitable and specific measures to safeguard the fundamental rights and the interests of the data subject
notes: confirmed: 9(2)(j) derogates 9(1)

a_anchor: High-risk AI systems shall technically allow for the automatic recording of events (logs) over the lifetime of the system.
b_anchor: kept in a form which permits identification of data subjects for no longer than is necessary
a_condition: over the lifetime of the system
b_condition: in a form which permits identification of data subjects for no longer than is necessary for the purposes for which the personal data are processed
notes: The AI Act says keep logs for the system's whole lifetime; the GDPR says do not keep personal data longer than needed. If the logs contain personal data you cannot fully obey both at once - this is the flagship conflict. The way out is already in the graph: the archiving exception (pt_e#s1) and pseudonymisation under Art 32.

a_anchor: The information shall be provided in writing, or by other means, including, where appropriate, by electronic means
b_anchor: The controller shall take appropriate measures to provide any information... in a concise, transparent, intelligible and easily accessible form
b_condition: in a concise, transparent, intelligible and easily accessible form, using clear and plain language, in particular for any information addressed specifically to a child
notes: judgement call: s1 (form of provision: writing/electronic) vs s2 (intelligibility manner) are distinct requirements; IRI granularity collapses both to MakeAvailable x Communication

a_condition: Taking into account the purposes of the processing, including by means of providing a supplementary statement

b_condition: Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons
